article_title: Pleural cerebrospinal fluid shunting causing trapped lung: A respiratory physician's approach to management and prevention
label: Fig. 1
caption: (A) Chest radiograph taken before thoracentesis demonstrating a large left-sided pleural effusion. (B) Post-thoracentesis chest radiograph demonstrating an air-fluid level over the left lung apex, compatible with hydropneumothorax, and minimal left lung expansion.

article_title: Pleural cerebrospinal fluid shunting causing trapped lung: A respiratory physician's approach to management and prevention
label: Fig. 2
caption: (A) Post-thoracentesis computed tomography scan showing left hydropneumothorax, left lung collapse, and rightwards mediastinum shift.
